screenshot_note: Puppeteer timed out on Network.enable (protocolTimeout). System resource-constrained — same failure class as review 93. Review conducted on source code alone: App.jsx (62.7 KB), Office.jsx (77.9 KB), App.css, Office.css. Git log unavailable (forkpty error); fixed-items determination based on code inspection only.
theme: Eleven items carried from review 93, zero fixed. Three new bugs found: a time-bomb JSON.parse that Sofia flagged in QA #85 (nine review cycles old across two reviewers), an ErrorBoundary message that contradicts its own Retry button, and a night sky that is starless while midnight shows stars. The coffee corner accessibility issue is worse than review 93 documented: not one CSS pseudo-element injection but four. The office accumulates technical debt faster than it pays it down.
fixed_since_last_review_note: Git log was inaccessible (forkpty resource error). Code inspection of every flagged line from review 93 found all items unchanged. Zero confirmed fixes between review 93 and review 94.

ideas.title: JSON.parse(raw) Unguarded on VIBE_HISTORY_KEY — Silent Crash Loop in useEffect
ideas.description: App.jsx:1114 — const history = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : []. The safeRead() call on line 1113 handles SecurityError and iOS private mode. JSON.parse is NOT protected. If localStorage["bloberto-vibe-history"] contains corrupted data (stale app version format, user-edited storage, partial write from page unload, browser extension), JSON.parse throws SyntaxError. This fires inside a useEffect — NOT during render — so the ErrorBoundary cannot catch it. React 18 logs an unhandled exception; vibeHistory stays empty; setVibeHistory on line 1118 never runs. Every subsequent vibe change re-enters the same effect, re-reads the same corrupted string, and throws again — infinite silent exceptions until the user manually clears localStorage. Sofia flagged this as MEDIUM in QA review #85. It has survived nine review cycles across two reviewers. Fix: wrap in try-catch: const history = (() => { try { return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : [] } catch { return [] } })(). Better: add safeParseJSON(str, fallback) to src/utils/safeSave.js alongside safeSave and safeRead — the pattern is established, the utility belongs there, five lines. WHY: safeRead exists because storage access can fail silently. JSON.parse is the second half of that same contract. Both halves need protection.

ideas.title: WorkerCard Back Panel Key Uses Delimiter-Free Concatenation — Same Bug Fixed Elsewhere (Carry-Over #93)
ideas.description: App.jsx:395 — workerHistory.map((e) => (<div key={e.timestamp + e.type + (e.worker || "")}...)). ActivityLogEntry at line 157 was fixed in review 92 to use a template literal with underscore delimiters. The WorkerCard back panel was missed. String concatenation without delimiters produces collision-prone keys: two different timestamp+type combinations can produce the same concatenated string. The probability is low but the inconsistency with the already-fixed line 157 is active confusion for every developer reading both. Fix: key={e.timestamp + "_" + e.type + "_" + (e.worker ?? "system")}. Two-character change. WHY: the fix was applied to half the codebase in review 92. Finish it. Still open after review 93.

ideas.title: standupWorkers Character Render Skip Uses .includes() While Desk Check Uses .some() — Inconsistent Split (Carry-Over #93)
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1443 vs 1571 — Two checks that must always agree use different identity strategies. Line 1443: const isStandup = occ && standupWorkers.some(sw => sw.id === occ.worker.id) — id-safe, fixed in review 92. Line 1571: if (standupWorkers.includes(occ.worker)) return null — still uses reference equality. If object references diverge (normalization, spread, new field added in a data shape change), the desk correctly skips the standup class while the Character still renders at the desk — producing a visual duplicate where the same worker appears at their desk AND at the conference table simultaneously. Review 93 escalated this to must. Fix: change line 1571 to if (standupWorkers.some(sw => sw.id === occ.worker.id)) return null. WHY: before the partial fix, both checks were wrong together. Now they disagree, which is harder to detect and debug than a consistent error. Still open.

ideas.title: ErrorBoundary Message Tells Users to Refresh While a Retry Button Exists — Contradictory Instructions
ideas.description: App.jsx:18-19 — The ErrorBoundary renders "Something went wrong — refresh the page" alongside a Retry button that calls this.resetError. These two instructions contradict each other. The text says: take one action (browser refresh, which clears React state and all loaded data). The button offers a different action (in-place error reset, which preserves all fetched worker data). A user who reads the text first will manually refresh, losing loaded data, when clicking Retry would recover in under a second with data intact. The message was written before the Retry button existed — componentDidCatch and resetError were added in review 92. The message was never updated. Fix: change line 18 to "Something went wrong." and let the button speak for itself. Or: "Something went wrong — click Retry, or refresh if it persists." WHY: user-facing copy that contradicts a UI element is a UX bug. The Retry button is the better recovery path. The copy should guide users toward it, not away.

ideas.title: ConferenceTable SVG Filter Has Hardcoded ID — Document-Scope Collision Risk (Carry-Over #93)
ideas.description: Office.jsx:941 — <filter id="conf-fire-glow" ...>. SVG filter IDs are document-scoped. ConferenceTable is rendered once today so no collision exists yet. CharacterAvatar already solves this correctly at line 230: const gradId = useId() — the pattern is established in this codebase. If ConferenceTable is ever rendered twice (kiosk mode, test harness, split-view layout), the second instance silently references the first instance filter definition, or applies no glow at all. Fix: const glowFilterId = useId() at the top of ConferenceTable, replace id="conf-fire-glow" with id={glowFilterId}, update the url() reference on line 933 to url(#). Three lines. WHY: CharacterAvatar knows why global SVG IDs are dangerous. ConferenceTable should too. Still open after review 93.

ideas.title: Window Sky Is Deep Purple Night at 8pm While Header Greets Good Evening — Two Hours Out of Sync (Carry-Over #93)
ideas.description: Office.jsx:869 vs App.jsx:1163-1171 — The window sky gradient activates deep purple night at h >= 20 (8pm). The header greeting changes from Good evening to Burning the midnight oil only at h >= 22 (10pm, the isAfterHours threshold). Result: at 8pm and 9pm, the office window looks like deep midnight while the header says Good evening, boss. The sky is the loudest ambient storytelling element in the view. When it says midnight while the header says evening, the immersion breaks for two hours every evening. Fix: shift the night sky from h >= 20 to h >= 22. Extend the golden-hour gradient (currently h=17-19) to cover h=17-21. WHY: the sky must agree with the greeting. Still open after review 93.

ideas.title: Luna's 94 Creative Reviews Are Ghost Events — Still Not in the Activity Log (Carry-Over Reviews 83-93)
ideas.description: data/activity.json — Luna has completed 94 creative reviews. Zero appear in the activity log. Sofia QA reviews through #85 are present. Carlos fixes appear. Vibe transitions appear. Luna does not exist in her own product record. Flagged in twelve consecutive reviews (83-94). The action: after writing ideas.json, append an entry to activity.json with type=complete, worker=Luna, message=Creative review #94 complete, timestamp=ISO now. Same pattern Sofia uses after each QA. Sixty seconds. The same sixty seconds not taken after reviews 83 through 93. WHY: the activity log is the team shared memory. If Creative Direction is invisible in that log, it does not exist in the record. Twelve reviews. Same sentence.

ideas.title: staleMins Computed Twice in the Same Block With Different Names — Still Open (Carry-Over #92, #93)
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1434 and 1440 — Two separate Math.floor(staleDiffMs / 60000) computations exist 6 lines apart. Line 1434: const mins = ... inside the staleDeskTitle IIFE. Line 1440: const ghostMins = ... for tier classification. Same value, two names, two computations. Fix: const staleMins = isStaleWorking ? Math.floor(staleDiffMs / 60000) : 0 before both uses. Replace mins and ghostMins with staleMins. One computation, no ambiguity about whether they could differ (they cannot — both floor the same staleDiffMs). WHY: two names for the same value in a 20-line block implies they might mean different things. They never can. Open since review 92.

ideas.title: Night Sky at h=20-23 Has No Stars While Midnight Sky at h=0-4 Does — Storytelling Discontinuity
ideas.description: Office.jsx:873 and 882-888 — isMidnight is defined as hour >= 0 && hour <= 4. Stars are rendered only inside the isMidnight block. The night sky gradient (h >= 20 && h <= 23) is visually identical (deep purple #4c1d95 to #312e81) but completely empty: no stars, no detail. At 11pm the window is a featureless dark rectangle. At 1am it has four blinking stars. The window looks more alive after midnight than before, which is backwards. Fix: change the star rendering condition to include isMidnight || hour >= 20. Optionally show slightly fewer stars at night than midnight for gradation. WHY: the sky gradient already acknowledges that night exists at 8pm. If the sky acknowledges night, it should look like night.

ideas.title: handleCharClick Over-Specified Dependency Array — Four Stable Deps Obscure the One Real Dep (Carry-Over #93)
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1257 — }, [setPingedId, setPingReaction, pingTimerRef, pingReactionTimerRef, onWorkerClick]). Four of the five deps are stable by React contract: setPingedId and setPingReaction are useState setters (guaranteed stable); pingTimerRef and pingReactionTimerRef are useRef objects (same reference for component lifetime). They will never trigger a re-creation. But their presence tells every reader this callback might recreate when setPingedId changes — it never does. The one dep that genuinely matters (onWorkerClick, a prop) is buried in the noise. Fix: }, [onWorkerClick]). WHY: dependency arrays are documentation. An array of four stable values plus one real dep documents nothing and obscures the one thing worth knowing. Still open after review 93.

ideas.title: workers.json Task Field Is Stale by Five Reviews — Luna's Entry Shows Creative Review #89
ideas.description: workers.json — Luna entry: status=idle, task=Creative review #89 — 3 fixes implemented by Bloberto, updated_at=2026-03-11T03:05:00Z. This is review #94. Five reviews behind. Also still missing id, role, and emoji fields (workers-json-missing-role-id carry-over). Luna worker card in the dashboard shows she last completed review #89. App.jsx:277 WORKER_TAGLINES for luna says Creative velocity is still velocity — that tagline is ironic when the worker card moves at zero velocity. Fix: update workers.json with id=luna, role=Creative, emoji=moon, name=Luna, status=working, task=Creative review #94, progress=75, started_at and updated_at as current timestamps. WHY: if the dashboard is the team live monitor, the Creative Director card should not be frozen in the past.

ideas.title: ETA Badge Shows Stale Predictions Without Decay — ~2m Left After 45 Minutes at 95% (Carry-Over #93)
ideas.description: App.jsx:235 — const eta = (progress > 5 && progress < 100 && workingMins > 1) ? Math.round((workingMins / progress) * (100 - progress)) : null. Linear extrapolation is sound when a task is moving. When a worker is stuck at 95% with updated_at frozen 45 minutes ago, the formula returns ~2 minutes while the duration badge says Working for 46m. The contradiction is visible on the same card at the same time. Fix: after computing eta, check if (worker.updated_at && eta !== null && (Date.now() - new Date(worker.updated_at).getTime()) > eta * 60 * 1000 * 1.5) set eta = null, or render an overdue indicator. WHY: false precision is worse than no precision. The ETA is the most prominent metric on the card and currently lies during exactly the situation when users most want accurate information. Still open after review 93.

ideas.title: quoteBonus Wrap Has No Ceremony — Tenth Consecutive Creative Review Without a Fix
ideas.description: App.jsx:1471 — setQuoteBonus(p => p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1). The tagline counter reaches 8/8 and resets to 1 in complete silence. Flagged in creative reviews 83 through 93 and now 94 — twelve total review cycles including QA. The fix infrastructure is entirely present: safeSave, setCompletionToast, the sparkle class system. The missing branch when p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length: call safeSave(footer-all-taglines-seen, 1), setCompletionToast with a milestone text and gold color, add footer-sparkle--complete class to freeze the sparkle gold, then reset. Twenty lines maximum. WHY: ten creative reviews. The tagline hunt is the only active discovery mechanic the product ships. A hunt that ends in silence is not a hunt. It is an accidental loop. The wires are present. Someone connect them.

ideas.title: CSS ::after Injects Four Vibe-Specific Coffee Labels — All Invisible to Screen Readers and Ctrl+F (Carry-Over #93, Expanded)
ideas.description: Office.css:1673-1677 — Review 93 flagged only the TRIPLE SHOT injection for crushing vibe. The full scope is four variants all using ::after pseudo-elements: crushing adds TRIPLE SHOT, on-fire adds STRONG. NOW., slow-day adds ...zzz, after-hours adds (decaf? jk). All four: invisible to NVDA+Chrome, cannot be selected or copied, not findable by Ctrl+F, absent from the accessibility tree. Fix: remove all four ::after rules. In Office.jsx at line 1552, conditionally render a span with a COFFEE_SUFFIXES lookup keyed by vibe. Add aria-label on the corner container for screen reader context. Same four labels, in the DOM, readable by everyone. WHY: visible content should live in the DOM. Review 93 flagged one label. All four need the same fix.

ideas.title: idleBubble Is a 90-Character Inline Date.now() Computation in JSX — Still Open (Carry-Over #93)
ideas.description: Office.jsx:813 — The idle bubble emoji is selected by a 90-character inline expression that evaluates the pool array twice, calls Date.now() inline on every render, and buries the logic in JSX where it cannot be tested. The idleBubble state is a boolean (true/false) but the actual emoji is determined at render time, not at set time. Fix: change setIdleBubble(true) to setIdleBubble(pickIdleBubble(worker.id)) where pickIdleBubble is a pure function above the component that selects the emoji string. Store the string in state. Render {idleBubble} directly. WHY: the delight of idle micro-bubbles deserves to be implemented in a way that can be read, tested, and changed without an archaeology dig through 90 characters of inline JSX. Still open after review 93.

_still_open_from_prior.note: App.jsx:395 — key={e.timestamp + e.type + (e.worker || "")}. Promoted to must in review 93. ActivityLogEntry was fixed in review 92. WorkerCard back panel missed. Confirmed unchanged in review 94.
Office.jsx:1443 uses .some(), line 1571 uses .includes(). Partial fix from review 92 created two checks that now disagree. Escalated to must in reviews 93 and 94. Confirmed unchanged.

_still_open_from_prior.note: App.jsx:1114 — JSON.parse(raw) without try-catch on VIBE_HISTORY_KEY. Sofia flagged as MEDIUM in QA #85. Luna escalates to must in review #94. Confirmed unchanged.
Office.jsx:941 — hardcoded SVG filter id. CharacterAvatar uses useId() at line 230. Confirmed unchanged.
Office.jsx:869 night purple starts h>=20. App.jsx:1163 isAfterHours starts h>=22. Two-hour disconnect. Confirmed unchanged.
Twelve consecutive reviews (83-94). Zero creative review entries in data/activity.json. Sofia QA reviews through #85 present. Luna does not exist in her own product record.
Office.jsx:1434 and 1440 — two Math.floor(staleDiffMs / 60000) calls with different names. Open since review 92. Confirmed unchanged.
App.jsx:1471 — counter wraps from 8 to 1 silently. Ten consecutive creative reviews. Twelve total cycles. Infrastructure is complete. This is a will problem.

_still_open_from_prior.status: still open — scope expanded in review 94
_still_open_from_prior.note: Office.css:1673-1677 — four vibe-specific labels via CSS ::after, not just TRIPLE SHOT. All four have same accessibility problem.
Office.jsx:1257 — four stable deps alongside the one real dep. Confirmed unchanged.
App.jsx:235 — linear ETA with no staleness check. Confirmed unchanged.
Office.jsx:813 — 90-character inline Date.now() expression in JSX. Confirmed unchanged.

_still_open_from_prior.status: still open — task field also stale by 5 reviews
_still_open_from_prior.note: workers.json — Luna entry missing id, role, emoji. Task field shows Creative review #89, five reviews stale as of review #94.
